runner.reportConsumer: release evidence and adopter plugin publishing review
runner.outputContract: registry, manifest, digest, signature, permission, compatibility, malicious-path, and failure-isolation results are represented as stable JSON cases

rows.publishRequirement: Registry entries must declare name, remote, version, protocol, checksum, source, manifest, dry-run requirement, and permission evidence.
rows.blockDecision: Block publication when registry metadata, checksum, source, protocol, manifest, dry-run, or permissions are incomplete.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep the previous registry entry pinned until the publisher republishes a complete registry record and conformance report.

rows.publishRequirement: Manifests must declare compatibleVersions, capabilities, filesystem:write-relative permissions, requiresDryRun, and publisher rationale.
rows.blockDecision: Block publication when manifest permissions, capabilities, compatibility, or dry-run declarations are unsupported.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Disable the plugin release or pin the previous manifest when permission, dry-run, compatibility, or capability evidence regresses.

rows.publishRequirement: Published plugins must provide sha256 digest, signature provenance, source repository, and release-note provenance for adopter review.
rows.blockDecision: Reject digest mismatches and hold publication when signature provenance or source context is missing.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Disable the candidate release and keep the last digest-matched version available for rollback.

rows.publishRequirement: Protocol compatibility must cover old, current, future-plus-current, and future-only windows for a protocol 1 host.
rows.blockDecision: Reject old-only and future-only plugins while accepting current or future-plus-current plugins only after other conformance checks pass.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Require host upgrade evidence or republish with protocol 1 compatibility before install.

rows.publishRequirement: Template directories must declare schema, name, version, protocol, entrypoints, permissions, checksum, and source.
rows.blockDecision: Block template publication when schema, entrypoints, permission, checksum, source, or dry-run evidence is incomplete.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep the previous template pack pinned until template schema, entrypoint, permission, checksum, source, and isolation evidence pass.

rows.surface: malicious path and no partial writes
rows.publishRequirement: Plugin and template effects must reject malicious paths, symlink traversal, digest mismatch, permission escape, and partial writes before host mutation.
rows.blockDecision: Reject filesystem escapes and partial writes as publish blockers, not warnings.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Disable plugin effects and keep the previous digest-matched artifact when failure isolation evidence regresses.

adopterPublishingContract.policy: Adopters may publish or install third-party plugins only after registry, manifest, digest, signature, permission, compatibility, malicious-path, and failure-isolation evidence all pass.

publishBlockers.adopterAction: Block publication when registry metadata, manifest fields, dry-run requirements, source, checksum, or permission declarations are incomplete.
publishBlockers.rollbackAction: Keep the previous registry entry pinned until the publisher republishes a complete manifest and conformance report.

publishBlockers.adopterAction: Reject plugins that do not negotiate protocol 1 with the current host, even when their registry entry is otherwise complete.
publishBlockers.rollbackAction: Require host upgrade evidence or republish with current protocol compatibility before install.

publishBlockers.adopterAction: Reject digest mismatches and require signature provenance in release notes before trusting third-party plugin binaries.
publishBlockers.rollbackAction: Disable the plugin release and keep the last digest-matched version available for rollback.

publishBlockers.adopterAction: Reject plugins that request permissions outside filesystem:write-relative, attempt path escape, or leave partial writes on failure.
publishBlockers.rollbackAction: Keep plugin effects disabled and require a clean conformance rerun before allowing filesystem mutation.

rows.blockDecision: Reject old-only and future-only plugins before registry promotion.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Republish with protocol 1 compatibility or keep the previous plugin version pinned.

rows.blockDecision: Block publication when signature provenance or the trusted OIDC source is missing.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Disable the candidate release until the publisher attaches trusted signature provenance.

rows.blockDecision: Reject downloaded bytes when the registry checksum does not match the sha256 digest.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep the previous digest-matched artifact pinned and rerun conformance before cache promotion.

rows.blockDecision: Reject sources outside the HTTPS github.com allowlist before publication.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Move the source to an allowlisted repository or keep the previous registry entry pinned.

rows.blockDecision: Reject template packs without contractVersion 1 or required template metadata.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Pin the previous template contract or republish with contractVersion 1.

rows.blockDecision: Reject plugin effects when any path, digest, permission, or patch failure would leave partial writes.
rows.rollbackOrEscalation: Disable plugin effects and keep the previous digest-matched artifact active until isolation evidence passes.

supplyChainRows.compatibilityWindow: Registry entries must publish current protocol compatibility and reject old-only or future-only protocol windows before plugin execution.
supplyChainRows.permissionBoundary: Registry metadata must link to a manifest that declares least-privilege filesystem:write-relative permissions and dry-run support.
supplyChainRows.digestOrChecksum: Registry checksum must be sha256 and must match downloaded plugin bytes before cache promotion.
supplyChainRows.sourceRequirement: Registry source must identify the publisher repository or release source used for adopter review.
supplyChainRows.isolationRequirement: Registry validation must fail before plugin effects run when checksum, source, protocol, manifest, or permissions are incomplete.
supplyChainRows.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep the previous registry entry pinned until checksum, source, protocol, manifest, permission, and compatibility evidence pass.

supplyChainRows.compatibilityWindow: Manifest compatibility must include protocol 1 for the current host and reject future-only manifests without host upgrade evidence.
supplyChainRows.permissionBoundary: Manifest permissions are limited to filesystem:write-relative and must include a publisher rationale before publication.
supplyChainRows.digestOrChecksum: Manifest execution depends on registry digest verification and must not bypass checksum validation.
supplyChainRows.sourceRequirement: Manifest review must be tied to the registry source field and publisher release notes.
supplyChainRows.isolationRequirement: Unsupported capabilities or permission escapes must be rejected before any generated file or patch is applied.
supplyChainRows.rollbackOrEscalation: Disable the plugin release or pin the previous manifest when permission, dry-run, compatibility, or digest evidence regresses.

supplyChainRows.compatibilityWindow: Template directories must declare protocol 1 before being used by current gofly generators.
supplyChainRows.permissionBoundary: Template directories may only request filesystem:write-relative and must keep outputs relative to the target project root.
supplyChainRows.digestOrChecksum: Template checksum must be sha256 and stable for the published template directory contents.
supplyChainRows.sourceRequirement: Template source must identify the publisher repository or release source used for adopter review.
supplyChainRows.isolationRequirement: Template copy and generated files must reject symlink traversal, escaping paths, and partial writes on failure.
supplyChainRows.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep the previous template pack pinned until schema, entrypoint, permission, checksum, source, and isolation evidence pass.

supplyChainRows.compatibilityWindow: Failure isolation applies to old, current, and future protocol windows before any plugin effect is promoted.
supplyChainRows.permissionBoundary: Permission escapes outside filesystem:write-relative are publish blockers and must not be downgraded to warnings.
supplyChainRows.digestOrChecksum: Digest mismatch must fail closed before local cache promotion or generated output application.
supplyChainRows.sourceRequirement: Failure reports must preserve source and release-note context so adopters can identify the rejected publisher artifact.
supplyChainRows.isolationRequirement: Malicious paths, symlink traversal, digest mismatch, permission escape, and partial writes must leave no committed host changes.
supplyChainRows.rollbackOrEscalation: Disable the plugin or template release and keep the previous digest-matched version available when isolation evidence fails.

cases.failurePolicy: registry entries missing checksum, source, protocol, manifest, dry-run, or permissions fail before publication
unsupported capabilities, unsupported permissions, and incompatible manifests fail before plugin effects run
old-protocol-only plugins are rejected for a protocol 1 host
current protocol plugins may run only after manifest, digest, and permission checks pass
future-capable plugins must keep protocol 1 behavior active until the host upgrades
future-only plugins are rejected until the host understands the future protocol
downloaded plugin bytes must match the registry sha256 digest and fail closed before cache promotion
publisher release notes must record signature provenance before publishing third-party plugins
permissions outside filesystem:write-relative are rejected before plugin effects run
file and patch paths must stay relative to the project root and reject symlink traversal
plugin output application must reject partial writes when any file or patch fails
